title: The Hands of Ms. Reed
introKo:
집 앞에 새로 생긴 마사지 스튜디오, 첫 방문. 관리사 Ms. Reed가 뭉친 곳을 유난히 정성껏 풀어 주는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.
introEn:
Your first visit to the little massage studio on your street. Perfectly wholesome.
...on the surface.

quiz.q: Ms. Reed가 목 옆에서 특히 정성껏 풀어 준 것은 무엇이었나요?

0000.name: 조심스러운 첫 손님
0000.en: You asked her to be gentle, kept every touch light, thanked her, and slipped quietly out the door. Ms. Reed remembers you as the one who never once asked for more. "Some knots take many visits," she thinks, "and yours will simply take their time."
0000.ko: 살살 해 달라 부탁했고, 손길은 내내 가볍게 두었고, 고맙다는 말만 남기고 조용히 문을 나섰죠. Ms. Reed는 당신을 한 번도 '더'를 청하지 않은 손님으로 기억해요. "어떤 뭉침은 여러 번 와야 풀리죠. 당신 것은 천천히 풀릴 거예요." 그녀는 그렇게 생각해요.

0001.name: 조용히 다시 오는 사람
0001.en: You were gentle, careful, patient — and then, at the very end, you booked next week before she could offer. "The shy ones are the ones who quietly never leave," Ms. Reed tells herself. Your Friday evenings belong to her now.
0001.ko: 살살, 조심조심, 참을성 있게. 그러다 마지막 순간에, 그녀가 권하기도 전에 다음 주를 예약했죠. "수줍은 손님이 사실은 조용히 안 떠나는 손님이에요." Ms. Reed가 혼잣말해요. 이제 당신의 금요일 저녁은 그녀의 것이에요.

0010.name: 딱 한 번의 그 한마디
0010.en: You stayed soft and polite all evening, except for a single moment when you whispered "right there, don't stop." Ms. Reed heard it, and she has not forgotten it. "Quiet water," she smiles, "with one deep current underneath."
0010.ko: 저녁 내내 부드럽고 예의 발랐죠. 딱 한 순간, "바로 거기요, 멈추지 마세요"라고 속삭인 것만 빼면요. Ms. Reed는 그 말을 들었고, 아직 잊지 않았어요. "잔잔한 물, 그 밑에 깊은 물살 하나." 그녀가 미소 지어요.

0011.name: 끝에서 눈뜬 단골
0011.en: For half the hour you were shy and mild, and then something woke up: "right there," and "same time next week." Ms. Reed loves that turn most of all. "They arrive so careful," she says, "and leave already counting the days."
0011.ko: 수업 절반은 수줍고 얌전했는데, 어느 순간 무언가가 깨어났죠. "바로 거기요", 그리고 "다음 주 같은 시간에요." Ms. Reed는 그 반전을 제일 좋아해요. "그렇게 조심스럽게 왔다가, 벌써 날짜를 세면서 돌아가요." 그녀가 말해요.

0100.name: 말없이 세게
0100.en: You asked to be treated gently, but when it counted you told her not to hold back. Then you went quiet again, thanked her, and left. "He wanted it firm," Ms. Reed notes, "and told no one but me."
0100.ko: 살살 해 달라더니, 정작 중요한 순간엔 봐주지 말라고 했죠. 그러곤 다시 조용해져서, 고맙다는 인사만 남기고 떠났어요. "세게 원했으면서, 나한테만 말했네요." Ms. Reed가 속으로 새겨요.

0101.name: 취향을 아는 단골
0101.nameEn: The One Who Knows What He Likes
0101.en: Gentle at first, then firm when it mattered, and booked again on your way out. You learned your own taste fast. "Beginners rarely know what they want," Ms. Reed says. "You already do."
0101.ko: 처음엔 부드럽게, 중요할 땐 세게. 그리고 나가는 길에 다시 예약까지. 자기 취향을 참 빨리 알아챘죠. "초보는 보통 자기가 뭘 원하는지 몰라요. 당신은 벌써 알더군요." Ms. Reed가 말해요.

0110.name: 속을 다 보인 손님
0110.en: You began politely, but soon you wanted it firm, and "right there, don't stop." Then, oddly, you left without a word about coming back. Ms. Reed stood by the door a while. "All that," she wondered, "and he walks away?"
0110.ko: 예의 바르게 시작했지만, 곧 세게 원했고, "바로 거기요, 멈추지 마세요"까지 갔죠. 그런데 이상하게, 다시 오겠단 말 한마디 없이 떠났어요. Ms. Reed는 한참 문가에 서 있었어요. "그렇게까지 해 놓고, 그냥 가 버리네요?"

0111.name: 한 시간 만에 물오른
0111.en: You walked in asking for gentle and walked out asking for more — firm, "right there," next week, all of it. "Some bodies just need one hour to remember what they like," Ms. Reed laughs. Yours remembered fast.
0111.ko: 살살 해 달라며 들어와서는, 더 세게, "바로 거기", 다음 주까지 — 전부 청하며 나갔죠. "어떤 몸은 딱 한 시간이면 뭘 좋아하는지 기억해 내요." Ms. Reed가 웃어요. 당신 몸은 빨리도 기억했네요.

1000.name: 큰소리 새내기
1000.en: "I can take a lot," you announced — then asked if the pressure was okay, took it gently, and thanked her at the door. Ms. Reed found it charming. "Big words, careful body," she smiles. "The sweetest kind of first-timer."
1000.ko: "저 잘 참아요"라고 큰소리쳐 놓고, 정작 압은 괜찮냐고 묻고, 살살 받고, 문 앞에서 고맙다 인사했죠. Ms. Reed는 그게 귀엽대요. "말은 크고 몸은 조심스럽고 — 제일 사랑스러운 첫 손님이에요." 그녀가 미소 지어요.

1001.name: 큰소리치고 다시 오는
1001.en: You boasted you could take a lot, stayed careful through the hour, then booked next week before she asked. "He talks big and comes back bigger," Ms. Reed says. Your Fridays are hers now.
1001.ko: 잘 참는다고 큰소리치더니, 한 시간 내내 조심스러웠고, 그러곤 그녀가 묻기도 전에 다음 주를 예약했죠. "말은 크게 하고, 더 크게 다시 와요." Ms. Reed가 말해요. 이제 당신의 금요일은 그녀의 것이에요.

1010.name: 허세와 진심 사이
1010.en: You came in bold, softened in the middle, then blurted "right there, don't stop," and left without a booking. Ms. Reed keeps your knots waiting anyway. "He will be back," she says. "Bodies remember what mouths forget."
1010.ko: 당당하게 들어와서, 중간엔 얌전해졌다가, "바로 거기요, 멈추지 마세요"라고 툭 뱉고는, 예약도 없이 떠났죠. Ms. Reed는 그래도 당신의 뭉친 자리를 남겨 둬요. "다시 올 거예요. 입이 잊어도 몸은 기억하거든요." 그녀가 말해요.

1011.name: 떠오르는 단골
1011.en: Confident from hello, one bold "right there," and a booking before the towel was folded — only the pressure did you leave to her hands. "Sure of everything but the how," Ms. Reed smiles. "That is exactly why they come back to me."
1011.ko: 첫인사부터 자신만만, "바로 거기" 한 번, 그리고 수건을 개기도 전에 예약까지 — 오직 세기만 그녀 손에 맡겼죠. "다 확신하면서 방법만 나한테 맡겨요." Ms. Reed가 미소 지어요. "바로 그래서 다들 나한테 다시 오죠."

1100.name: 세게 왔다 조용히 간
1100.en: You strode in bold and asked for it firm — then went calm, patient, and slipped away with a polite thank-you. "He came in like a storm," Ms. Reed says, "and left like a good night of sleep."
1100.ko: 당당하게 들어와 세게 해 달라더니, 이내 차분하고 참을성 있어졌고, 공손한 인사와 함께 조용히 사라졌죠. "폭풍처럼 들어와서, 푹 잔 잠처럼 나갔어요." Ms. Reed가 말해요.

1101.name: 세게, 그리고 또
1101.en: Bold at the door, firm on the table, and booked again before you left — the one thing you let her lead was the patient middle. "He knows what he wants," Ms. Reed says, "and he wants next Friday too."
1101.ko: 문 앞에선 당당하게, 테이블에선 세게, 그리고 나가기 전에 또 예약까지 — 그녀에게 맡긴 건 참을성 있던 중간뿐이었죠. "자기가 뭘 원하는지 아는 사람이에요. 다음 금요일까지 원하네요." Ms. Reed가 말해요.

1110.name: 다 가지고 사라진
1110.en: Bold, firm, "right there, don't stop" — you asked for everything and got it, then left with only a thank-you. Ms. Reed watched the door swing shut. "A perfect evening," she sighs, "gone home too soon."
1110.ko: 당당하게, 세게, "바로 거기요, 멈추지 마세요" — 원하는 걸 다 청하고 다 받아 놓고는, 고맙다는 말만 남기고 떠났죠. Ms. Reed는 닫히는 문을 바라봤어요. "완벽한 저녁이었는데, 너무 빨리 집에 가 버렸네요." 그녀가 한숨지어요.

1111.name: 소문난 금요일의 단골
1111.en: Bold from hello, firm all through, "right there, don't stop," and hooked before the hour was up. By spring the whole street knows Ms. Reed has one client who never misses a Friday. She only smiles when they ask why. "Some knots," she says, "are worth coming back for."
1111.ko: 첫인사부터 당당하게, 내내 세게, "바로 거기요, 멈추지 마세요", 그리고 한 시간이 끝나기도 전에 중독됐죠. 봄이 오자 온 거리가 알아요. Ms. Reed에게 금요일을 한 번도 거르지 않는 단골 하나가 있다는 걸요. 왜냐고 물으면 그녀는 그저 웃어요. "어떤 뭉침은, 다시 올 만한 가치가 있죠." 그녀가 말해요.

normal.text: Friday evening. It is your first visit to the little massage studio on your street. Warm lamps glow, and the air smells of oil and herbs.
"Welcome. I am Ms. Reed," the masseuse says with a soft smile. "Your body looks so tired. Don't worry — you are in good hands tonight."
She warms a little oil between her palms. "Your first time?" she asks. "Then just lie down, relax, and let me do all the work."

options.en: Yes, my first time. Please be gentle.
options.reaction: "Gentle to start," she smiles. "We can go deeper once your body is ready."

options.en: It is my first time, but I can take a lot.
options.ko: 처음이긴 한데, 저 좀 잘 참아요.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" She laughs softly. "Then let us see how much you can take."

normal.text: You lie face down on the warm table. "Breathe out," Ms. Reed says. "The more you let go, the better this feels."
Her warm hands press along your shoulders. "You are so tense," she murmurs. "Nobody has been taking care of you."

options.en: Is this pressure okay for a beginner?
options.ko: 초보한테 이 정도 세기면 괜찮은 거죠?
options.reaction: "Perfect for now," she says. "Tell me the moment you want more."

options.en: Don't hold back. I like it firm.
options.ko: 봐주지 마세요. 저 세게 하는 거 좋아해요.
options.reaction: "A bold one," she laughs. "Good. I do my best work when nobody tells me to stop."

normal.text: She stops at one spot near your neck. "Here," she says softly. "Right here. Feel that knot? I will work on it until it lets go."
"A little deeper now," she whispers, leaning her weight in. "Do not fight me. Just breathe, and let it open up."

options.en: That is the good kind of sore.
options.reaction: "That is the knot letting go," she says. "Stay still. We are almost there."
"I had no plan to stop," she murmurs. "Not while one knot is left."

normal.text: More warm oil. Her hands move slow and sure down your back. Time goes soft, and the world outside this room disappears.
"There — you are finally letting go," Ms. Reed says. "See? A body only opens up when it trusts the hands."

options.en: I feel like a new person. Thank you.
options.ko: 완전히 새사람이 된 것 같아요. 고맙습니다.
options.reaction: "That is my favorite part," she smiles. "Sending you home lighter than you came."

options.en: Same time next week? I am already hooked.
options.ko: 다음 주 같은 시간에요? 저 벌써 중독됐어요.
options.reaction: "They always come back," she says. "Once my hands find your knots, you never forget."

normal.text: Later, you sit by the window with warm tea, loose and calm. Your first time, you decide, will not be your last.

hard.text: Friday evening draws you to the little massage studio that just opened on your street. Warm lamps glow along the walls, and the air is heavy with the scent of oil and dried herbs.
"Welcome. I am Ms. Reed," the masseuse says, her voice low and unhurried. "Your shoulders are carrying the whole week, I can tell. Relax — you are in very good hands tonight."
She warms a pool of oil slowly between her palms. "Your first time here?" she asks. "Then simply lie down, surrender the weight, and allow me to do every bit of the work."

options.en: Yes, my very first time. Please be gentle with me.
options.reaction: "Gentle to begin with," she smiles. "We can always go deeper once your body decides it is ready."

options.en: First time, yes — but I can take quite a lot.
options.ko: 처음 맞아요. 근데 저 꽤 잘 참는 편이에요.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" She laughs under her breath. "Then let us find out exactly how much you can take."

hard.text: You settle face down onto the warm table. "Breathe all the way out," Ms. Reed murmurs. "The more completely you let go, the better every moment of this will feel."
Her warm hands travel slowly across your shoulders and press in. "You are wound so tightly," she observes quietly. "Clearly nobody has been taking proper care of you."

options.en: Is this much pressure alright for a beginner?
options.ko: 초보한테 이 정도 압이면 괜찮은 걸까요?
options.reaction: "Ideal for now," she assures you. "Just say the word the instant you want a little more."

options.en: Please don't hold back. I prefer it firm.
options.ko: 봐주지 말고 해 주세요. 저 센 거 좋아해요.
options.reaction: "A bold one," she laughs softly. "Wonderful. I always do my finest work when no one is telling me to stop."

hard.text: Her fingers halt at a single spot beside your neck. "Here," she says almost in a whisper. "Right here. Can you feel that stubborn knot? I intend to stay on it until it finally lets go."
"A little deeper now," she breathes, leaning her weight through her palms. "Don't tense against me. Simply breathe, and let everything open up on its own."

options.en: That is the good kind of sore.
options.reaction: "That is the knot beginning to surrender," she says. "Hold still. We are very nearly there."
"Stopping was never part of my plan," she murmurs. "Not while a single knot remains."

hard.text: More warm oil, and her hands glide slow and certain the length of your back. Time itself turns soft. The entire world beyond this quiet room simply disappears.
"There — you are finally letting go," Ms. Reed says warmly. "You see? A body only truly opens up once it learns to trust the hands upon it."

options.en: I feel like an entirely new person. Thank you.
options.ko: 완전히 다른 사람이 된 기분이에요. 감사합니다.
options.reaction: "That is my favorite part of all," she smiles. "Sending you home lighter than you first walked in."

options.en: Same time next week? I am already hooked.
options.ko: 다음 주 같은 시간 어때요? 저 벌써 중독됐어요.
options.reaction: "They always come back," she says knowingly. "Once my hands have found your knots, the body never quite forgets."

hard.text: Later you sit by the window cradling warm tea, loose and calm. This first time, you quietly decide, will certainly not be your last.

across.en: from one side to the other

announced.en: said in a clear, strong way (past of announce)

beginner.en: a person who is just starting

between.en: in the space of two things

blurted.en: said suddenly without thinking (past of blurt)

boasted.en: said proudly you can do something (past of boast)

breath.en: air you take in; under your breath means very quietly

breathe.en: to take air in and out

breathes.en: takes air in and out; says softly

clearly.en: in a way easy to see or understand

client.en: a person who pays for a service

current.en: water that moves in one direction

do.en: a helper word for questions and not

evening.en: the end part of the day

exactly.en: in a precise way, just right

fingers.en: the long parts of your hand

firsttimer.en: a person doing something for the first time

half.en: partly; one of two equal parts

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

herbs.en: plants used for their smell or flavor

instant.en: a moment; the instant means as soon as

kind.ko: 종류 (the safest kind: 제일 안전한 부류)

knot.en: a tight, hard spot in a muscle

know.en: to have something in your mind

leave.en: to go away; to let stay

letting.en: allowing; let go means to relax

lie.en: to put your body flat, as on a bed

light.en: brightness; the shine of a lamp or fire

massage.en: rubbing the body to relax it

middle.en: the center point of a place

mouths.en: the parts of the face you speak with

move.en: one action of the body in dance

ms.en: a polite title before a woman's name

neck.en: the part between your head and shoulders

offer.en: to say you will give or do something

oil.en: a smooth liquid rubbed on the skin

onto.en: to a place on top of

palms.en: the inside surfaces of your hands

person.en: a human; in person means face to face

pool.en: a small amount of liquid gathered together

prefer.en: to like one thing more than another

quite.en: fully; not quite means almost but not

scent.en: a smell, usually a nice one

sending.en: making someone or something go somewhere

shoulders.en: the body parts beside your neck

slipped.en: past form of slip; put in quietly

something.en: a thing you do not name

stopping.en: bringing to an end; from stop

strode.en: past form of stride; walked with long steps

studio.en: a room where art is made

surrender.en: to give up and let go

swing.en: to move or shut in a curve

taking.en: taking a step: moving one foot

taste.en: to try food in your mouth

tea.en: a hot drink made from leaves

touch.en: a touch means a little bit

towel.en: a cloth for drying your body

travel.en: to move from one place to another

us.ko: 우리를 (the two of us: 우리 둘)

visit.en: to go to see a person or place

visits.en: times you go to a place

voice.en: the sound you make when speaking

welcome.en: a friendly hello to a guest

window.en: glass in a wall you see through

wound.en: wound tightly means very tense (past of wind)

wink.oil: 따뜻하게 데운 오일과 미끄러지는 손 — 마사지의 기본이에요. 다만 그 단어를 고른 것도, 굳이 데운 것도 사실이죠.
wink.take: "얼마나 참을 수 있는지 볼까요"는 압을 얼마나 견디느냐는 이야기예요. 압 이야기라고, Ms. Reed는 분명 말할 거예요.
wink.firm: "세게 하는 거 좋아해요", "봐주지 마세요" — 강도 요청이에요. 강도요. 정말로요.
wink.knot: "여기 뭉쳤네요, 풀릴 때까지 붙잡고 있을게요"는 근육 뭉침 이야기예요. 어디까지나 근육이요.
wink.deeper: "조금만 더 깊이"는 압을 더 준다는 뜻이에요. 원래 마사지에서 그렇게 말하니까요. 원래요.
wink.spot: "바로 여기", "바로 거기" — 뭉친 자리를 정확히 짚었다는 말이에요. 그 자리요.
wink.hooked: "저 벌써 중독됐어요"는 마사지가 시원해서 하는 말이에요. 시원해서요.
wink.trust: "몸은 손을 믿어야 열려요"는 긴장을 놓는 이야기예요. 손을 믿는 이야기이기도 하고요.

winkEn.oil: Warm oil and gliding hands are the basics of any massage. It is also true that she chose the word, and true that she warmed it first.
winkEn.take: "Let us see how much you can take" is about how much pressure you can bear. Pressure, Ms. Reed will insist, and only pressure.
winkEn.firm: "I like it firm" and "don't hold back" are requests about strength. About strength. Truly.
winkEn.knot: "Here is a knot, I will stay on it until it lets go" is about a tight muscle. Strictly a muscle.
winkEn.deeper: "A little deeper now" means a little more pressure. That is simply how massage talk works. Simply.
winkEn.spot: "Right here," "right there" means she found the exact tight place. That place.
winkEn.hooked: "I am already hooked" is what you say when a massage feels wonderful. Because it felt wonderful.
winkEn.trust: "A body opens up when it trusts the hands" is about releasing tension. And about trusting the hands, too.
